senses.definition: (of a person) feeling really happy or satisfied

examples.x: After winning the championship, the ecstatic team captain stood on top of the world, holding the trophy high above their head.
As the newlywed couple danced under the starry sky, they felt on top of the world, surrounded by love and happiness.
With their dream job offer in hand, Sarah felt on top of the world, knowing that her hard work had paid off.

senses.definition: to make someone's ordinary or dull day more enjoyable or memorable

examples.x: Her kind words really made my day.
Winning the contest made his day.
A surprise visit from her best friend made her day.
Her success story made their day and inspired them.

senses.definition: knowledge that gives someone relief as they did nothing wrong and should not feel guilty

examples.x: After apologizing and making reparations for his mistakes, he felt a sense of relief and walked away with a clear conscience.
Despite facing temptation, she followed her moral compass and made the right choice, leaving her with a clear conscience.
He could boldly speak his mind during the meeting because he had thoroughly prepared and had a clear conscience about his intentions and actions.
Knowing that she had always treated others with kindness and fairness, she went to bed each night with a clear conscience and a peaceful mind.

word_list.pronunciation: æz kˈʌmftəbəl æz ɐn ˈoʊld ʃˈuː

senses.definition: used to describe something that causes one to feel very much at ease

examples.x: After years of playing together, the band members were so in sync that performing on stage felt as comfortable as an old shoe.
Sitting in his favorite armchair with a good book in hand, he was as comfortable as an old shoe, completely at ease and content.

word_list.word: (as|) snug as a bug in a rug
word_list.pronunciation: æz snˈʌɡ æz ɐ bˈʌɡ ɪn ɐ ɹˈʌɡ

senses.definition: used to refer to something that is very soothing and comforting

examples.x: Curled up by the fireplace with a book and a cup of tea, she felt as snug as a bug in a rug.
The baby drifted off to sleep, swaddled in a soft blanket, looking as snug as a bug in a rug.
Wrapped in a warm scarf and a cozy sweater, she ventured out into the winter chill, feeling snug as a bug in a rug.

senses.definition: to feel really happy, excited, or satisfied

examples.x: After a successful job interview, he walked out of the office with confidence, feeling like he was floating on air.
After winning the championship, the team walked on air, celebrating their hard-earned victory.
The breathtaking view from the mountaintop made her feel like she was floating on air, in awe of the beauty of nature.
When her favorite band performed her favorite song live, she was filled with pure happiness and felt like she was floating on air.

senses.definition: filled with extreme happiness, excitement, or satisfaction

examples.x: His face lit up with a big smile as he read the heartfelt letter from his best friend, leaving him feeling tickled pink.
The comedian's hilarious jokes had the entire audience in stitches, leaving them tickled pink throughout the entire show.
When she saw the adorable puppy, she couldn't help but squeal with delight and was absolutely tickled pink.

senses.definition: used when someone wishes to continue doing something as much or as long as they desire

examples.x: After a long week of work, she decided to relax at the beach, reading her favorite book and soaking up the sun to her heart's content.
The buffet at the wedding reception was filled with delicious delicacies, and guests indulged in the wide array of dishes, eating to their heart's content.
With the day off from responsibilities, he spent the entire afternoon playing video games, losing himself in the virtual world to his heart's content.

senses.definition: to bring a positive change to someone's mood or emotional state

examples.x: Participating in a yoga class and focusing on deep breathing raised her spirits and brought a sense of calm and
Reconnecting with an old friend and reminiscing about cherished memories raised his spirits and brightened his day.
Taking a long walk on the beach and feeling the gentle breeze on his face lifted his spirits and brought a sense of peace.
The heartfelt words of encouragement from her best friend lifted her spirits and gave her the strength to face the challenges ahead.
The surprise visit from her family during a difficult time lifted her spirits and reminded her of the love and support she had.

senses.definition: in a state where one is experiencing outmost joy and happiness

examples.x: After receiving a promotion at work, she was in seventh heaven, ecstatic about her new role and increased responsibilities.
As the final notes of the concert resonated through the hall, the audience was in seventh heaven, captivated by the breathtaking performance.
As the newlyweds danced their first dance, they were in seventh heaven, lost in each other's arms, and filled with the bliss of love.
Surrounded by loved ones and basking in the warm glow of birthday wishes, she felt in seventh heaven, overwhelmed with happiness and gratitude.

senses.definition: in a good mood because of enjoying what one does or being in a place or situation that one likes

examples.x: She was in her element while leading the team discussion.
As a chef, he is in his element in the kitchen.
The artist was in her element when painting outdoors.
He felt in his element during the coding competition.
Surrounded by books, the librarian was truly in her element.

senses.definition: used to indicate a state of great happiness, bliss, or contentment

examples.x: After a long week of work, she was in hog heaven, lounging by the pool with a refreshing drink in hand.
As the music blared and the crowd cheered, the concertgoers were in hog heaven, dancing and singing along to their favorite songs.
Surrounded by shelves of rare books, the bibliophile was in hog heaven, happily lost in a world of literature.
With a pile of presents to unwrap, the child was in hog heaven on their birthday, eagerly tearing through the colorful wrapping paper.
With a plate full of her favorite desserts, she was in hog heaven, savoring each sweet bite.

word_list.word: (as|) happy as a clam (at hot water|)
word_list.pronunciation: æz ɔːɹ hˈæpi æz ɐ klˈæm æt hˈɑːt wˈɔːɾɚɹ ɔːɹ

senses.definition: (of a person) very pleased or satisfied

examples.x: After receiving a promotion at work, she was as happy as a clam.
Surrounded by loved ones on her birthday, she felt happy as a clam at hot water.

senses.definition: tremendously excited about something

examples.x: After winning the championship, she was on cloud nine.
Getting engaged to the love of her life put her on cloud nine.
He couldn't stop smiling after his surprise birthday party; he was on cloud nine the entire evening.
Receiving the acceptance letter to her dream university put her on cloud nine.

senses.definition: to be extremely happy or excited about something

examples.x: After months of hard work, he was over the moon when he received a promotion at work.
They were over the moon when they discovered they were expecting a baby.
When she found out she won the lottery, she was over the moon with joy.
The kids were over the moon when they saw the theme park.

word_list.pronunciation: lˈaɪf ɪz ɐ bˈoʊl ʌv tʃˈɛɹɪz

senses.definition: used to say that life is full of joy and happiness
to feel comfortable or confident in a place, situation, or activity

examples.x: As soon as she stepped into her cozy living room, she instantly felt at home.
In the bustling kitchen, the experienced chef felt at home, orchestrating the culinary symphony with ease.
Simon is at home on the stage, performing in front of an audience.
They were at home discussing their favorite hobbies during the gathering.

word_list.word: like a dog with two tails
word_list.pronunciation: lˈaɪk ɐ dˈɑːɡ wɪð tˈuː tˈeɪlz

senses.definition: very pleased and satisfied with something good that has happened to one

examples.x: His face lit up with excitement, and he couldn't stop smiling, thrilled like a dog with two tails.
When she received the scholarship, she was jumping up and down like a dog with two tails.

word_list.word: (as|) happy as a flea in a doghouse
word_list.pronunciation: æz hˈæpi æz ɐ flˈiː ɪn ɐ dˈɑːɡhaʊs

senses.definition: used to describe someone who is very happy and satisfied

examples.x: Despite the tiny hotel room, she was as happy as a flea in a doghouse, simply thrilled to be on vacation.
He may have a small office, but he's happy as a flea in a doghouse, enjoying his work and the cozy environment.

senses.definition: extremely happy and content

examples.x: After receiving the job offer, she was as happy as Larry, jumping for joy.
He danced and sang, happy as Larry, when his favorite team won the championship.
With the sun shining and a picnic spread out before them, the family felt happy as Larry in the park.

senses.definition: a person who is very content or satisfied

examples.x: After a long day of hiking, they sat by the campfire as happy campers, sharing stories and laughter.
Despite the rain, she remained a happy camper, singing and dancing at the music festival.
Even with all the challenges, he's always a happy camper, finding joy in the simple things.

senses.definition: (of a person) very pleased, delighted, or satisfied with something
